FR-001.title: New /speckit.companion.living-sync command registered in the extension manifest

FR-002.title: Sync collects working-tree changes (uncommitted, untracked, deletions) plus commits since each spec's baseline

FR-003.title: Grouping reuses the existing resolver membership rules — no second derivation

FR-004.title: Each affected capability gets the update-not-regenerate flow scoped to its changed files, automatically

FR-005.title: Sync reports synced/skipped capabilities and leaves spec edits uncommitted

FR-006.title: Sync is opt-in and non-halting; disabled config means clean no-op

FR-007.title: drift gains opt-in --working mode; default mode byte-identical; never-fails and counts contract kept

FR-008.title: Sync grouping IS the working-tree drift derivation — one fact, one derivation

FR-009.title: Sidebar action 'Sync living specs from my changes' dispatches the command with existing gating

FR-010.title: Never-committed capability specs are skipped with a reason, never redrafted

context: constraint: never-fails exit-0 drift contract; no extension.yml version bump; update-not-regenerate
intent: One command syncs every affected living spec from current changes, including uncommitted work, in a single pass
expectations: No redraft/bootstrap of never-committed specs (living-adopt owns that)
No auto-commit of spec edits — they stay in the user's working tree
No change to default drift output or the fold-back pipeline path
approach: Extend drift.py with an opt-in --working mode (baseline-to-working-tree diff plus untracked files) and make that the single grouping derivation. A new hand-authored /speckit.companion.living-sync command consumes its JSON, applies the update-not-regenerate flow per drifted capability, reports synced/skipped, and leaves spec edits uncommitted. A Living Specs view title action dispatches the command.

decisions.why: drift already owns membership, exemption, baselines, skip reasons, and the never-fails contract; report and sync must not disagree
decisions.rejected: a dedicated sync grouping script (second derivation)

decisions.decision: Working set = git diff <baseline> unioned with ls-files --others --exclude-standard
decisions.why: one diff covers commits-since + staged + unstaged + deletions with no double-report; untracked needs the standard plumbing call

decisions.decision: Hand-authored command body mirroring the living-* family, reusing the #475 update-not-regenerate voice
decisions.why: living-* bodies live outside node assembly; one voice for update semantics across viewer and command

decisions.decision: Sidebar action = view/title item on Living Specs at navigation@3, dispatching the slash command
decisions.why: identical pattern and companion-install gating as the adopt action; the dispatched text is a real slash command
decisions.rejected: extension-built multi-capability prose prompt (would re-derive grouping)

plan.summary: drift.py --working becomes the shared grouping engine; new living-sync command applies per-capability updates and reports; sidebar title action dispatches it
plan.key_finding: living-* command bodies are hand-authored (no node assembly), and check-command-emissions requires the new command in extension.yml, both doc tables, and re-emitted dev-install symlinks

tasks.summary: 14 tasks across 6 phases: foundational drift --working + tests, US1 command body + registration, US2 docs, US3 sidebar action + tests, polish docs + validation

implement.summary: drift --working shipped with 11 tests; living-sync command authored+registered+emitted; sidebar sync action wired and locked; both doc sets updated; all gates green

T001.did: Added opt-in --working mode to drift.py: baseline-to-worktree diff plus untracked, working-scoped tracked-set, JSON working field, working-tree header note; default git commands unchanged

T002.did: 11 working-mode drift tests: uncommitted/untracked/deletion drift, dedupe, exempt filter, skip counts, tracked via uncommitted context, JSON mode flag, default-mode immunity to a dirty tree, exit-0

T003.did: Authored the living-sync command body: drift --working --json as the plan, per-capability update-not-regenerate scoped to drifted files, honest skips pointing at living-adopt, uncommitted-by-design report

T004.did: Registered speckit.companion.living-sync in extension.yml provides.commands (no version bump)

T005.did: Re-emitted dev-install agent commands; emissions gate now fails only on the two doc tables T008/T009 close

T006.did: Documented --working in the living-drift command body and pointed the report at living-sync as its write-side twin

T007.did: Contributed speckit.livingSpecs.sync command (sync icon) and its Living Specs view/title item at navigation@3

T010.did: Registered the sync handler dispatching /speckit.companion.living-sync via executeSlashCommand (adopt pattern)

T011.did: Sync dispatch test: registered in the command set, dispatches the slash command with title, never via executeInTerminal

T012.did: Locked the sync action's title in the manifest test title table

T008.did: spec-kit README: command-table row (five, was four), --working in the drift section, new Syncing-from-your-changes section

T009.did: commands.md: families table + living-sync entry + --working on the drift entry

T013.did: Two user-voice [Unreleased] entries: the one-pass sync and the drift --working mode

T014.did: Root README Living Specs paragraph + docs/sidebar.md title-actions line and Living Specs section now carry the sync action

T015.did: Validated: tsc clean, 1726 jest + 466 python tests pass, shape-parity/command-emissions/package-manifest gates OK, live drift --working smoke run
